image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: How engineers dreamed of turning Egypt's Qattara Depression into an endless power source
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Workers digging a railway tunnel in Sweden uncovered six shipwrecks spanning 5 centuries
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Britain's canals were built for industry, but they are now helping the wildlife survive
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Invasive plants may be boosting mosquito numbers in lower-income areas
item.description: Recent studies indicate that invasive trees in economically disadvantaged neighborhoods of Baltimore and Washington D.C. are contributing to a rise in mosquito populations. The nonnative leaves serve as an excellent nourishment source for mosquito larvae, promoting faster growth and larger size, which ultimately enhances their ability to transmit diseases. This finding underscores the significance of plant quality over quantity in managing mosquito outbreaks.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Hidden in Egypt's Dakhla Oasis, 7,000-year-old rock carvings are slowly disappearing
item.description: Ancient rock art in Egypt's Dakhla Oasis is facing significant decline. Natural erosion and shifting weather patterns are gradually wearing away these ancient markings. Modern graffiti and human activity also add new scars to the desert art. Archaeologists are documenting the fragile rock art to preserve its details. Efforts aim to record this history before it is permanently erased.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Elon Musk: "Being an entrepreneur is like eating glass and staring into…" - brutally honest lesson on entrepreneurship, resilience and the hidden reality behind building a business
item.description: Elon Musk described entrepreneurship as eating glass and staring into death's abyss. He shared this honest perspective during a 2010 event after Tesla's public offering. Musk detailed December 2008 as a profoundly difficult period in his life. He explained 'staring into the abyss' as startup failure awareness and 'eating glass' as difficult problem-solving. Resilience and adaptation are crucial for founders navigating unpredictable business obstacles.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Sequoias' survival hinges on fire management; researchers urge expanding controlled burns to protect forests
item.description: Giant sequoias faced unprecedented losses in recent wildfire seasons. A NASA-funded study shows controlled burns dramatically improve their survival chances. Mature trees in treated forests were four times less likely to die. This practice minimizes fuel and allows fires to burn with lower intensity. Expanding prescribed burning programs is crucial for preserving these ancient giants.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: She walked over 200 miles in the snow to feed starving army
item.description: Polly Cooper, an Oneida woman, journeyed 200 miles to deliver essential food supplies to the starving Continental Army. She prepared corn and taught soldiers how to cook, saving many lives during a harsh winter. Cooper refused payment for her actions, receiving only a shawl and bonnet as thanks. Her vital contributions were preserved through oral tradition and later documented by historians. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: TOI Lifestyle Desk

item.title: 25,000-year-old cave discovery reveals ancient Australian rituals
item.description: In Cloggs Cave, archaeologists have discovered evidence of ancient GunaiKurnai grass burning rituals that spanned 25,000 years. The findings, which include microscopic plant remnants, ash layers, and a remarkable standing stone, shed light on the deep-rooted spiritual connection that these ancient communities fostered with nature. This enduring practice highlights the intricate relationship between the environment and their spiritual ceremonial activities.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: MIT AI researcher warns replacing Gen Z with automation could be making a billion-dollar mistake
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘Place of democracy’: Hitler’s birthplace converted into police station in Austria
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: "Wild pursuit of fame and fortune": Candace Owens tears into Erika Kirk, claims Charlie Kirk was her "ticket to more fame"
item.description: Candace Owens intensified her public feud with Erika Kirk, accusing her of pursuing fame and fortune throughout her life. In a podcast, Owens claimed Charlie Kirk was Erika's "ticket to more fame" and argued a recent article criticizing her instead exposed Erika's character. She also questioned Erika's motives, saying her focus had always been on public attention, money, and influence.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: A backyard chicken owner thought hens were eating the missing eggs, but later found...
item.description: Chicken keepers report sightings of snakes feasting on their eggs within nesting boxes. These serpents are drawn to the warmth of coops and the presence of stored chicken feed. Experts advocate using humane deterrents instead of harsh chemicals for effective long-term management. Regularly collecting eggs and securing feed bins can effectively keep these unwanted guests at bay. Enhancing coops can safeguard eggs while allowing snakes to maintain the ecosystem's balance.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: From oil to atoms: Donald Trump to open nuclear door to Saudis
item.description: The Trump White House is set to announce a significant nuclear cooperation deal with Saudi Arabia. This agreement allows for extensive civilian nuclear cooperation and uranium enrichment on Saudi soil. Critics express concerns about nuclear proliferation and regional security implications. The US administration argues this approach counters Russian and Chinese influence in the region. Lawmakers will scrutinize the deal, which may weaken existing non-proliferation safeguards.
item.dc:creator: Chidanand Rajghatta

item.title: Indian trucker with legal California driver's licence arrested over $3 million stolen powder
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Amazon fires peaked during the 2024 drought. A year later, burned area hit its lowest level since records began in 1985
item.description: Amazon forest fires reached a historic low in 2025 after a severe 2024 drought. Shifting weather patterns and reduced deforestation contributed to this significant decline. Community awareness grew after the previous year's widespread destruction and damage. President Lula's administration prioritized forest protection, leading to lower deforestation rates. However, a potential super El Niño event threatens future progress and increased fire risks.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: In 1955, Hawaii released a cannibal snail to control another invasive snail, but the predator…
item.description: Hawaii introduced a predatory snail in 1955 to control pests, which then devastated native tree snails. Scientists now operate a snail emergency room to protect the remaining species. These colorful kāhuli snails were once abundant and culturally significant to Hawaiians. Captive breeding is slow, and efforts focus on predator-proof fences for reintroduction. Conservationists strive to prevent further extinctions, highlighting a global trend of human-induced endangerment.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: '3/10 — see me after class!': Tories mock new UK education secretary Lucy Powell over grammar gaffe
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Israeli reality TV star Yakir Levi dies after fall from 28th floor of Miami luxury hotel
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: A silent coral reef came back to life after scientists played recordings of healthy reefs underwater
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk
